title: Refactor LSP-016 (LSP): Subclass nullifies a parent class method by raising NotImplementedError.

detected_issue.description: Subclass nullifies a parent class method by raising NotImplementedError.
detected_issue.reasoning: Refused Bequest smell -> LSP violation because MockRequest explicitly mimics the urllib2.Request interface but raises NotImplementedError for the add_header method. This violates the substitution principle where a subtype (the mock) should be able to stand in for the base type (the real request) without throwing unexpected errors for standard interface methods.

fix_logic.summary: Implement add_header in MockRequest to satisfy the urllib2.Request interface.
fix_logic.rationale: The MockRequest class was raising NotImplementedError for add_header, which violates the Liskov Substitution Principle by breaking the expected interface contract. Implementing it to use add_unredirected_header ensures the mock behaves as expected by callers of the interface.

changes.replace_text:
def add_header(self, key, val):
        """
        This method is required by the urllib2.Request interface.
        """
        self.add_unredirected_header(key, val)

--- a/src/requests/cookies.py
+++ b/src/requests/cookies.py
@@ -76,10 +76,10 @@ class MockRequest:
         return self._r.headers.get(name, self._new_headers.get(name, default))
 
     def add_header(self, key, val):
-        """cookiejar has no legitimate use for this method; add it back if you find one."""
-        raise NotImplementedError(
-            "Cookie headers should be added with add_unredirected_header()"
-        )
+        """
+        This method is required by the urllib2.Request interface.
+        """
+        self.add_unredirected_header(key, val)
 
     def add_unredirected_header(self, name, value):
         self._new_headers[name] = value
